mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero since late 2008, the persistently low policy rate compresses equity discount rates and lowers the opportunity cost of holding stocks versus cash, producing a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or positive-return frequency during near-zero-rate periods is not statistically higher than the full-sample base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A discrete improvement in the unemployment rate, defined as a decline of at least 0.2 percentage points over the trailing three months, signals accelerating labor market healing that raises consumer income expectations and triggers a short-horizon equity rally as markets reprice growth prospects.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >= 0.2 percentage points over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised to 8.2% for 2012-07-01 vintage from 8.3%
failure_condition: Following triggered UNRATE declines of >=0.2pp, 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect reverses sign in a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in nonfarm payroll growth and industrial production growth indicates broad-based real-economy expansion across both labor and manufacturing sectors, a combination that historically precedes sustained equity gains over a quarterly horizon because it reduces recession risk premia priced into stocks; neither series alone captures this synchronized-growth signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1.0% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised to 134157 thousand for 2012-07-01 vintage
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are jointly met, 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than unconditional 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal in walk-forward backtests.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-interest-rate-policy regime, rising housing starts signal a strengthening consumer demand cycle that is disproportionately amplified because near-zero FEDFUNDS makes mortgage financing exceptionally cheap, so the housing-led wealth effect translates more strongly into forward equity gains than it would in a normal-rate environment.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 757 thousand units revised for 2012-06-01 vintage while FEDFUNDS at 0.16%
failure_condition: The positive relationship between HOUST momentum and 60-day forward SPX returns disappears or reverses once FEDFUNDS rises above 1%, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 during the ZIRP subsample.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden upside breach in CPI inflation above the Fed's implicit 2% comfort zone raises market expectations of premature policy tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 228.59 revised value for 2012-07-01 vintage
failure_condition: Following CPI breaches above 2.5% YoY, 20-day forward returns are not negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the historical relationship is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A steady positive trend in retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending, which supports corporate revenue expectations and produces a modest persistent positive drift in short-horizon S&P 500 returns as long as the trend continues, independent of any specific threshold or regime condition.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised to 389686 million dollars for 2012-07-01 vintage
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between RSAFS trend and 5-day forward returns stays below 0.02, or the sign of the correlation flips across two consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-to-mid cycle recovery regime following a recession, positive momentum in industrial production signals renewed manufacturing capacity utilization that precedes broader corporate earnings recoveries, generating positive forward equity returns over a monthly horizon; this relationship is materially weaker or absent in late-cycle or contraction regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.8713 revised value for 2012-07-01 vintage
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to produce a statistically significant positive coefficient on 20-day forward returns (p > 0.05) when restricted to NBER-dated recovery periods, or the sign flips during the recovery subsample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of an elevated but declining unemployment rate alongside a near-zero federal funds rate indicates the Fed is committed to prolonged accommodation despite labor slack, a policy-put dynamic that supports risk asset prices; forward equity returns are more strongly positive when both conditions hold simultaneously than when either holds alone, because markets price in extended low-rate duration only when labor slack persists.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.2% and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of the 2012-07-01 vintage
regime_hint: extended monetary accommodation amid labor slack
failure_condition: When both UNRATE and FEDFUNDS trigger conditions hold, 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional average (p > 0.05), or the joint-condition signal underperforms the single-series FEDFUNDS-only signal in out-of-sample tests.
